mnemonics: S.L.P. = Simply Line Plotting: Show how a variable's value changes over a sequential path.
keyPoints: A simple line plot displays information as a series of data points connected by straight line segments, ideal for showing trends over time or a continuous variable.
The x-axis typically represents the independent variable (e.g., time, sequence), while the y-axis represents the dependent variable being measured.
It is crucial to ensure the data points on the x-axis are ordered and evenly spaced to avoid misrepresenting the trend.
commonMistakes: Connecting points with a line when the x-axis data is categorical and not ordered, which can imply a sequence or trend where none exists.
Using a line plot for discrete data or data with very few points, where a bar chart or scatter plot might be more appropriate and less misleading.
